hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>, metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            state,\n            headers,\n            sid,\n            client_requested_score,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n            ) {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n            );\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    state: Arc<AppState>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // Extract session ID if provided\n    let sid = session_id;\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n    // should have identical shard layout)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n            primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n            ) {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n            );\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, primary_target\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n        query: body.q.clone(),\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add settings headers\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n        primary_target = %primary_target,\n        target_count = targets.len(),\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"multi-target search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\npub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        if !client_requested_score {\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private data\".to_string()),\n            offset: Some(0),\n            limit: Some(20),\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!({\"email\": \"user@example.com\"})),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".to_string()]),\n            ranking_score: {\n82\t    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n83\t    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n84\t    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n85\t *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n99\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n105\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n106\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n110\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n111\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n112\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n113\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n114\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n115\t        met\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Count how many groups }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n139\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n140\twhere\n141\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n142\t{\n143\t    Router::new()\n144\t (POST/PUT).\n245\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\n246\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n247\t    index: String,\n248\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n249\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n250\t    state: &AppState,\n251\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n252\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n253\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n254\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n255\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n256\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n257\t        ));\n258\t    }\n259\t\n260\t    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n261\t    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n262\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n263\t        // Check if the index is an alias\n264\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n265\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n266\t            // It was an alias\n267\t            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n268\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n269\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n270\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n271\t                    format!(\n272\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n273\t                        index\n274\t                    ),\n275\t                ));\n276\t            }\n277\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n278\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n279\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n280\t        } else {\n281\t            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n282\t            index.clone()\n283\t        }\n284\t    } else {\n285\t        index.clone()\n286\t    };\n287\t\n288\t    // 1. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n303\t    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n304\t    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n305\t    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n306\t    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n307\t\n308\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n309\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n310\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n311\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n312\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n313\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n314\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n315\t            ));\n316\t        }\n317\t\n318\t        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n319\t        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n320\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n321\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n322\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n323\t            ));\n324\t        }\n325\t\n326\t        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n327\t        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n328\t        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n329\t        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n330\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n331\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n332\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n333\t            ));\n334\t        }\n335\t\n336\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n337\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n338\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n339\t                format!(\n340\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n341\t                    i, primary_key\n342\t                ),\n343\t            ));\n344\t        }\n345\t    }\n346\t\n347\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard into each document\n348\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n349\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n350\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n351\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n352\t\n353\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n354\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n355\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n356\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n357\t        }\n358\t    }\n359\t\n360\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n361\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n362\t\n363\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n364\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n365\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n366\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n367\t    );\n368\t\n369\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n370\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n371\t\n372\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n373\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n374\t        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n375\t        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n376\t            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n377\t            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n378\t                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n379\t                    c.read().await\n380\t                })\n381\t            })\n382\t        });\n383\t\n384\t        // Use migration-aware routing\n385\t        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n386\t            shard_id,\n387\t            &topology,\n388\t            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n389\t        );\n390\t\n391\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n392\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n393\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n394\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n395\t            ));\n396\t        }\n397\t\n398\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n399\t\n400\t        for node_id in targets {\n401\t            let node = topology\n402\t                .node(&node_id)\n403\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n404\t\n405\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n406\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n407\t\n408\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n409\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n410\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n411\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n412\t            };\n413\t\n414\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n415\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n416\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n417\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n418\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n419\t                    }\n420\t                }\n421\t                Ok(resp) => {\n422\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n423\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n424\t                }\n425\t                Err(_) => {\n426\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n427\t                }\n428\t            }\n429\t        }\n430\t    }\n431\t\n432\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n433\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n434\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n435\t\n436\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n437\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n438\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n439\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n440\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n441\t        ));\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n445\t    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n446\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n447\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n448\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n449\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n450\t            acks >= quorum\n451\t        })\n452\t        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n453\t\n454\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n455\t    let miroir_task = state\n456\t        .task_registry\n457\t        .register_with_metadata(\n458\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n459\t            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n460\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n461\t        )\n462\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n463\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n464\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n465\t        ))?;\n466\t\n467\t    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n468\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n469\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n470\t            sid,\n471\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n472\t            first_quorum_group,\n473\t        ).await {\n474\t            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n475\t            tracing::error!(\n476\t                session_id = %sid,\n477\t                error = %e,\n478\t                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n479\t            );\n480\t        }\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n484\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n485\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n486\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n487\t            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n488\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n489\t            error: None,\n490\t            error_type: None,\n491\t            code: None,\n492\t            link: None,\n493\t        },\n494\t        degraded_groups,\n495\t    )\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n499\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n500\t    index: String,\n501\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n502\t    state: &AppState,\n503\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n504\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n505\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n506\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n507\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n508\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n509\t        ));\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n513\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n514\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n515\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n516\t            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n517\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n518\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n519\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n520\t                    format!(\n521\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n522\t                        index\n523\t                    ),\n524\t                ));\n525\t            }\n526\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n527\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n528\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n529\t        } else {\n530\t            index.clone()\n531\t        }\n532\t    } else {\n533\t        index.clone()\n534\t    };\n535\t\n536\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n537\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n538\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n539\t\n540\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n541\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n542\t    for id in &req.ids {\n543\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n544\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n545\t    }\n546\t\n547\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n548\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n549\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n550\t    );\n551\t\n552\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n553\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n554\t\n555\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n556\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n557\t        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n558\t\n559\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n560\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n561\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n562\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n563\t            ));\n564\t        }\n565\t\n566\t        for node_id in targets {\n567\t            let node = topology\n568\t                .node(&node_id)\n569\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n570\t\n571\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n572\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n573\t\n574\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n575\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n576\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n577\t            };\n578\t\n579\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n580\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n581\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n582\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n583\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n584\t                    }\n585\t                }\n586\t                Ok(resp) => {\n587\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n588\t                }\n589\t                Err(_) => {\n590\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n591\t                }\n592\t            }\n593\t        }\n594\t    }\n595\t\n596\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n597\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n598\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n599\t\n600\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n601\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n602\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n603\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n604\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n605\t        ));\n606\t    }\n607\t\n608\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n609\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n610\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n611\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n612\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n613\t            acks >= quorum\n614\t        })\n615\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n616\t\n617\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n618\t    let miroir_task = state\n619\t        .task_registry\n620\t        .register_with_metadata(\n621\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n622\t            Some(index.clone()),\n623\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n624\t        )\n625\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n626\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n627\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n628\t        ))?;\n629\t\n630\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n631\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n632\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n633\t            sid,\n634\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n635\t            first_quorum_group,\n636\t        ).await {\n637\t            tracing::error!(\n638\t                session_id = %sid,\n639\t                error = %e,\n640\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n641\t            );\n642\t        }\n643\t    }\n644\t\n645\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n646\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n647\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n648\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n649\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n650\t            error: None,\n651\t            error_type: None,\n652\t            code: None,\n653\t            link: None,\n654\t        },\n655\t        degraded_groups,\n656\t    )\n657\t}\n658\t\n659\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n660\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n661\t    index: String,\n662\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n663\t    state: &AppState,\n664\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n665\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n666\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n667\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n668\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n669\t\n670\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n671\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n672\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n673\t    );\n674\t\n675\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n676\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n677\t\n678\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n679\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n680\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n681\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n682\t\n683\t        match client\n684\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n685\t            .await\n686\t        {\n687\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n688\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n689\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n690\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n691\t                }\n692\t            }\n693\t            Ok(resp) => {\n694\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n695\t            }\n696\t            Err(_) => {\n697\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n698\t            }\n699\t        }\n700\t    }\n701\t\n702\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n703\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n704\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n705\t\n706\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n707\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n708\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n709\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n710\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n711\t        ));\n712\t    }\n713\t\n714\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n715\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n716\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n717\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n718\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n719\t            acks >= quorum\n720\t        })\n721\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n722\t\n723\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n724\t    let miroir_task = state\n725\t        .task_registry\n726\t        .register_with_metadata(\n727\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n728\t            Some(index.clone()),\n729\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n730\t        )\n731\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n732\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n733\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n734\t        ))?;\n735\t\n736\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n737\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n738\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n739\t            sid,\n740\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n741\t            first_quorum_group,\n742\t        ).await {\n743\t            tracing::error!(\n744\t                session_id = %sid,\n745\t                error = %e,\n746\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n747\t            );\n748\t        }\n749\t    }\n750\t\n751\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n752\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n753\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n754\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n755\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n756\t            error: None,\n757\t            error_type: None,\n758\t            code: None,\n759\t            link: None,\n760\t        },\n761\t        degraded_groups,\n762\t    )\n763\t}\n764\t\n765\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n766\t///\n767\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n768\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n769\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n770\t        .iter()\n771\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n772\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n773\t}\n774\t\n775\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n776\t///\n777\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n778\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n779\t///\n780\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n781\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n782\t    documents: &[Value],\n783\t    primary_key: &str,\n784\t    topology: &Topology,\n785\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n786\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n787\t\n788\t    for doc in documents {\n789\t        let pk_value = doc\n790\t            .get(primary_key)\n791\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n792\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n793\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n794\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n795\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n796\t                )\n797\t            })?;\n798\t\n799\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n800\t        shard_documents\n801\t            .entry(shard_id)\n802\t            .or_default()\n803\t            .push(doc.clone());\n804\t    }\n805\t\n806\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n807\t}\n808\t\n809\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n810\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n811\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n812\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n813\t        indexUid: None,\n814\t        status: None,\n815\t        error: resp.message,\n816\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n817\t        code: resp.code,\n818\t        link: None,\n819\t    }\n820\t}\n821\t\n822\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n823\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n824\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n825\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n826\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n827\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n828\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n829\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n830\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n831\t        )\n832\t    })?;\n833\t\n834\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n835\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n836\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n837\t\n838\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n839\t    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n840\t        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n841\t    }\n842\t\n843\t    Ok(builder\n844\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n845\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n847\t            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n848\t        ))?)\n849\t}\n850\t\n851\t/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\n852\tfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n853\t    (\n854\t        StatusCode::OK,\n855\t        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n856\t        Json(resp),\n857\t    )\n858\t        .into_response()\n859\t}\n860\t\n861\t#[cfg(test)]\n862\tmod tests {\n863\t    use super::*;\n864\t    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n865\t    use serde_json::json;\n866\t\n867\t    #[test]\n868\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n869\t        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n870\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n871\t\n872\t        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n873\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n874\t\n875\t        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n876\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n877\t\n878\t        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n879\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n880\t    }\n881\t\n882\t    #[test]\n883\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n884\t        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n885\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n886\t    }\n887\t\n888\t    #[test]\n889\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n890\t        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n891\t        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n892\t        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n893\t    }\n894\t\n895\t    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n896\t    //\n897\t    // Tests the reserved field matrix per plan §5:\n898\t    // - `_miroir_shard`: Always reserved (unconditional)\n899\t    // - `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n900\t    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n901\t\n902\t    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n903\t    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n904\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n905\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n906\t            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n907\t        )\n908\t    }\n909\t\n910\t    #[test]\n911\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n912\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n913\t        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n914\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n915\t        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n916\t        assert_eq!(err.error_type, miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n917\t    }\n918\t\n919\t    #[test]\n920\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n921\t        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n922\t        let field = \"_miroir_updated_at\";\n923\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n924\t            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n925\t            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", field),\n926\t        );\n927\t        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n928\t        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n929\t    }\n930\t\n931\t    #[test]\n932\t    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n933\t        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n934\t        let field = \"_miroir_expires_at\";\n935\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n936\t
